title: IPv6Header reconstruction uses wrong case for field names
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/headers.py:160-168

_ipv6_header() accesses fields with specific case:
  hop_limit=fields['IPv6.hop_limit'],
  traffic_class=fields['IPv6.traffic_class'],
  flow_label=fields['IPv6.flow_label'],

But _ipv6_fields() at lines 144-154 populates the dict with the same case:
  'IPv6.traffic_class': header.traffic_class,
  'IPv6.flow_label': header.flow_label,
  ...
  'IPv6.hop_limit': header.hop_limit,

And the rules use:
  FieldDescriptor('IPv6.hop_limit', 8, MO.IGNORE, CDA.VALUE_SENT),

All use lowercase, so this is consistent. However, decompress() returns field IDs from the rule definitions. The codec reads 'IPv6.hop_limit' from the rule and uses it as the dict key.

But wait - the rules use 'IPv6.hop_limit' (lowercase), _ipv6_fields uses 'IPv6.hop_limit' (lowercase), and _ipv6_header uses 'IPv6.hop_limit' (lowercase). This should be consistent.

Let me verify... Actually this looks OK. Let me check if there are any other field name mismatches.
